headline: Agent latency optimization and Model Context Protocol dominate the window as tooling for autonomous tool use breaks out.
body: Google Cloud Tech drove the day with two demonstrations cutting AI agent execution time by 80 percent through workflow parallelization, using OpenTelemetry for bottleneck identification and Antigravity IDE profiling. Model Context Protocol broke out across the same source as a protocol letting agents discover and chain tools without hardcoding, shown both explained against traditional APIs and deployed in an enterprise platform orchestrating Oracle databases and vector stores. On models, Anthropic's Sonnet 5 launched alongside criticism that it underperforms GLM-5.2 and Opus while costing more, and the White House reversed a two-week restriction on Anthropic models. OpenAI voices surfaced separately, with Mark Chen warning that the window for human oversight is narrowing and Greg Brockman describing a merger of chatbots and persistent agents.
